_readme: PROMOTED TEMPLATE (Annex of spec 016). The agent-card the JetVision ASM's Seller Proxy publishes off-chain on the Neuron-blind translator's behalf (015 FR-S96); the EIP-8004 (007) Identity Registry entry stores the agentId + the agentURI pointing to THIS document. STRUCTURE (015 FR-S20a): this IS the 003 agentURI registration file — its `services[]` follows the Neuron 003/004 schema and MUST carry the mandatory services: three neuron-topic (stdIn/stdOut/stdErr, 003 FR-R02/R08), one neuron-p2p-exchange (003 FR-R03), plus the 008 neuron-commerce service for the consumable feed (here: adsb; 015 FR-S70). An ERC-8004 explorer reads name/description + services[] (topics, p2p, commerce) WITHOUT SAPIENT. The additive blocks identity (the FR-S22 PeerID/key binding + on-chain 007 reference) and capabilities/statusDefinition/tasking/extensions (the SAPIENT Registration content = the ICD, FR-S20) + trust ride alongside. PROXY-vs-TRANSLATOR split: the Seller Proxy fills the Neuron-side blocks (services[] + identity) and owns the on-chain identity; the translator supplies the SAPIENT-content blocks. NOTE: this is the SAPIENT `adsb` service only — raw Mode-S (BEAST) is a SEPARATE non-SAPIENT feed with its OWN agent-card / proxy pair / identity (016 Out of Scope; 015 FR-S95). Replace every <...> placeholder. Illustrative: the NORMATIVE requirements are 016 FR-A-A02/A03 + 015 FR-S20/S20a/S24 + 003/004; where this skeleton conflicts with the verified DSTL protos, 016's 'Mapping — reconciliations' table, or 003/004, those GOVERN.
name: neuron-jv-bridge JetVision ASM
description: SAPIENT Autonomous Sensor Module: decodes the JetVision Air!Squitter's aircraftlist.json (ADS-B 1090, MLAT, UAT 978, FLARM/OGN 868 — unified) and emits SAPIENT DetectionReports with the neuron.adsb/1 extension. One ASM, one assembled-with-position service `adsb`; raw Mode-S (BEAST) is a separate service on its own proxy pair (016 Out of Scope), not this feed.

services.name: stdIn
services._note: Auditable 004 lane, inbound to the ASM: HLDMM->ASM control (RegistrationAck/Task/AlertAck), 015 FR-S12. Schema per 003 FR-R02 / 004 FR-T14.

services.name: stdOut
services._note: Routine ASM output: the in-session Registration replay, TaskAck, and the periodic StatusReport (015 FR-S12).

services.name: stdErr
services._note: Exceptional ASM signals: Alert and the SAPIENT Error message (015 FR-S12). NOTE: the emergency-squawk Alert is deferred (016 CL-J10); today only Error rides here.

services.name: p2p
services._note: How a consumer obtains the ASM multiaddr for the 009 p2p DetectionReport stream (003 FR-R03 / 004 FR-T17). The /sapient/detection/2.0.0 stream rides this p2p connection. The actual multiaddr is exchanged over the referenced topic, not stored here.

services.name: adsb
services._note: The consumable SAPIENT feed (008 FR-P01/P03, 015 FR-S70). DetectionReports ride /sapient/detection/2.0.0 on the 009 p2p stream reachable via the neuron-p2p-exchange service above. 016 fixes neither unit nor amount; free = amount '0'; interval '0' = one-time; settlement is deployment-specific. The four decoded sources (1090/978/868/MLAT) are SOURCES WITHIN this one service, not separate services (016 CL-J1).

identity._note: Additive Neuron block (outside the 003 service schema): the FR-S22 PeerID/key binding plus the on-chain 007 reference. peerId/publicKey is the trust anchor and MUST match the on-chain key; the Seller Proxy owns and binds it (015 FR-S94). agentId/identityRegistry name the on-chain 007 entry whose agentURI resolves to THIS document (015 FR-S24). A BEAST modes-raw feed of the same box has a SEPARATE identity (015 FR-S95).

capabilities._note: Additive SAPIENT-content block (the ICD = the SAPIENT Registration content, 015 FR-S20). An ERC-8004 explorer ignores it; a SAPIENT-aware consumer reads it.
capabilities._trackingNote: → SAPIENT mode_definition.scan_type = SCAN_TYPE_FIXED, tracking_type = TRACKING_TYPE_TRACK (016 FR-A-A02 / FR-A-D02): the RF footprint is fixed/omnidirectional, and a stable object_id (ULID) is persisted per airframe keyed on the ICAO hex — hex is both the ingest key and the identity key (no MAC-rotation problem as in 017).
capabilities._classificationNote: Top-level types are derived from the ADS-B/FLARM emitter category (cat): A0-A7/B1-B5/B7 -> Air Vehicle; B6 -> UAV; C1-C3 -> Surface Vehicle; FLARM F0-F15 -> Air Vehicle (016 FR-A-A04). The category detail rides as a native classification sub_class. There is NO friend/foe / affiliation here (a CoT/HLDMM concern, 018), per SAPIENT.
capabilities._altitudeNote: Geometric (GNSS) altitude (altg) -> Location.Z with datum WGS84_E (ellipsoid); when only barometric altitude (alt) is available it maps with datum WGS84_G (geoid, closest-available label) and both raw altitudes ride in adsb.geoAltFt / adsb.baroAltFt (016 CL-J13).

coverage.note: ADS-B/Mode-S/UAT/FLARM are received, not ranged; coverage is the RF reception footprint of the sensor, not a steerable FoV. Fill with the sensor's sited reception area if known. By default every locally HEARD contact is reported: own-receiver sources (src A/L/S/D, provenance=local) AND MLAT (src M/F, provenance=network — locally heard, position computed by the cooperative MLAT network this box contributes to). Only network-relayed OGN-server contacts (src=O, provenance=relayed — never heard by this box) are excluded unless re-admitted, since they fall outside this sensor's coverage (016 FR-A-D05/CL-J4). Each report's adsb.provenance records local|network|relayed|unknown.

statusDefinition.note: SAPIENT Registration.status_definition.status_interval (015 FR-S20): the cadence at which this ASM emits StatusReports (sensor health/coverage/mode) on the auditable 004 lane. This is the SAPIENT heartbeat cadence, NOT the Neuron 005 liveness heartbeat (015 FR-S31). The feedSource (live|replay|synthetic|placeholder) rides here as a native status[] entry (016 FR-A-E02). Replace PT10S with the sited cadence.

tasking.note: SAPIENT mode_definition.task — the controls/filters this ASM accepts (015 FR-S25/S26). CONTROL_STOP + CONTROL_START are a MANDATORY per-session baseline (015 FR-S29 / 016 FR-A-D10): a well-formed STOP MUST be acked and MUST cease DetectionReport emission (incl. lost-track) to THAT session within ceaseWindow; CONTROL_START resumes without a new Registration; neither affects any other consumer's stream. Per-session OUTPUT FILTERS accepted: region_definition + class_filter_definition. NO detection_report_rate decimation (matches 017, 016 CL-J7). An altitude-band filter via neuron.adsb/1 is PENDING (016 CL-J11) and not yet accepted — an altitude-band Task is rejected with UnsupportedTask until declared.
tasking.ceaseWindowNote: Max latency from a CONTROL_STOP TaskAck to DetectionReports ceasing on that session (015 FR-S29); bounded, hard ceiling PT5S.

extensions.note: Carries the aircraftlist payload that has no native SAPIENT slot (adsb.* objectInfo namespace): source/provenance/availability, callsign/registration/type/operator/route, squawk + emergency, the ADS-B integrity set (NACp/SIL/SDA/PIC/MOPS/trust), target status, EHS selected-values, met, and MLAT diagnostics. An HLDMM that does not load this schema ignores adsb.* entries per SAPIENT extension rules.

trust.note: ADS-B/Mode-S/UAT broadcasts are UNAUTHENTICATED and spoofable; this ASM carries NO signature and asserts no authenticity (016 US4). Position INTEGRITY is conveyed via the native location error (from NACp) and the adsb.* quality set (adsb.nacp/sil/sda/pic/mopsVersion, adsb.trustCount), which the HLDMM weighs. MLAT positions are server-computed (provenance=network) and carry a conservative synthesised error bound (~300 m, 016 FR-A-D08) since a network solve has no NACp. Unlike 017 there is no broadcast-signature path.
